weather.description: Forecast scheduling, paddle score, nearby water, and weather reliability.
risk_checks: Cache invalidation behavior is preserved on warm and cold paths.
Score normalization changes do not silently shift output ranges.
Scheduled function cron expressions remain unchanged unless explicitly updated.
Error fallbacks still emit correct confidence levels to the frontend.

commerce.description: Products catalog, checkout, payment intent, and order completion.
risk_checks: Stripe payment intent creation and confirmation flow is preserved.
Product vote rate limiting is not weakened or removed.
Image serving routes still enforce correct content-type headers.
No cart or order mutation is exposed without authentication.

kortex.description: Smart links, tenant auth, redirect handling, billing, and analytics.
risk_checks: Tenant isolation is preserved — no cross-tenant data leak is possible.
Public redirect paths stay strictly separate from admin-only mutations.
Auth claim checks (admin, kreator) are enforced before any data mutation.
Billing visibility surfaces remain read-only from tenant perspective.

kreator.description: Creator application intake, onboarding state machine, and admin review.
risk_checks: Application state transitions are validated server-side.
Admin-only endpoints verify the admin custom claim before acting.
Google OAuth flow endpoints are not exposed to unauthenticated callers.
Onboarding completion is idempotent and does not double-process.

kamera.description: Camera catalog, lens data, preset generation, and predeploy validation.
risk_checks: Preset generation respects skill-level branching for apprentice/enthusiast/professional.
Camera catalog provenance metadata is not silently stripped.
Lens compatibility data is preserved when catalog entries are updated.
Predeploy validation checks are not weakened to make the deploy pass faster.

kutz.description: Nutrition parsing, meal suggestion, food search, and Fitbit integration.
risk_checks: Fitbit OAuth token refresh logic is not broken by middleware changes.
Food parsing edge cases (missing macros, null values) still return safe defaults.
Meal suggestion ranking does not expose raw Firestore document keys.
Rate-limited endpoints remain protected after any middleware refactor.

shared.description: Cross-API middleware, auth guards, error handling, and smoke validation.
risk_checks: Middleware execution order is preserved across all mounted routers.
Error handler shape is consistent — clients must not receive raw stack traces.
Firebase Admin SDK initialization is not duplicated or re-initialized per-request.
CORS and security headers are enforced before any route logic executes.
